---
title: "upstream-data-cleanup-740 — notes"
date: 2026-05-19
format: html
---

## Diagnostic — fp32 corruption in the prior parquet

Loaded the dirty parquet, located bad cells, characterized the distribution.
See README.md `Root cause` section for the verdict.

```python
import pyarrow.parquet as pq, numpy as np
pf = pq.ParquetFile(
    "data/snapshots/7.40-2025-12-16/processed/player_features_prepatch/train.parquet"
)
# All bad cells live in row group 2 (rows 2,097,152–3,145,727).
# All bad cells are in column p1_smoothed_winrate_hero.
# All bad cells fall on date 2025-12-29 within match index range
#   [2,344,604, 2,504,113), randomly distributed at ~4% density.
# Values are uninitialized-memory-shaped (NaN, denormals, mixed-magnitude).
```

## Patch verification — clean parquet build

Once `run_all.sh` finishes step 1, the post-write re-read assertion in
`build_features.py:validate_table()` guarantees zero corrupted cells.

```python
import pyarrow.parquet as pq, numpy as np
for split in ("train", "val"):
    tbl = pq.read_table(
        f"data/snapshots/7.40-2025-12-16/processed/player_features_prepatch_clean/{split}.parquet"
    )
    for p in range(10):
        col = tbl.column(f"p{p}_smoothed_winrate_hero").to_numpy()
        assert np.all(np.isfinite(col)) and col.min() >= 0 and col.max() <= 1, p
print("clean parquet verified")
```

## Headline metric — equality-band check

```python
import json
m = json.loads(open(
    "experiments/2026-05-19-upstream-data-cleanup-740/metrics_transformer_plus_features.json"
).read())
print("val_auc:", m["val_auc"])
print("band [0.6467, 0.6487] holds:", m["equality_band_holds"])
print("delta vs parent (0.6477):",
      m["delta_combined_vs_transformer_plus_features_extended_740"])
```
